pi_items.metadata_value: Use GIMP's Solid Noise render filter to generate procedural noise texture
pi_items.verified_value: GIMP has a Solid Noise filter that generates procedural noise textures
pi_items.source: Official GIMP documentation at https://docs.gimp.org/2.10/en/gimp-filter-noise-solid.html confirms Solid Noise filter exists and generates grayscale procedural noise

pi_items.metadata_value: Filter location: Filters → Render → Clouds → Solid Noise
pi_items.verified_value: In GIMP 2.10, the filter is correctly located at Filters → Render → Clouds → Solid Noise
pi_items.source: Official GIMP 2.10 documentation at https://docs.gimp.org/2.10/en/gimp-filter-noise-solid.html and https://docs.gimp.org/2.8/en/plug-in-solid-noise.html confirm this menu path for versions 2.2-2.10

pi_items.verified_value: Canvas is created as 512x512 pixels with white color (255,255,255)
pi_items.source: setup_noise_task.sh uses ImageMagick command: convert -size 512x512 xc:white blank_canvas.png

pi_items.verified_value: Turbulence is a checkbox option that activates turbulent mode (not a numeric parameter with range 1-7)
pi_items.source: Official GIMP documentation confirms Turbulent is a checkbox boolean option, not a parameter with numeric range. The documentation states: 'checking this box will create a noise pattern with an increased chaotic nature'
GIMP source code and documentation show Detail range is 0-15. setup_noise_task.sh claims 1-15, which excludes the minimum value of 0

pi_items.verified_value: Solid Noise filter completely overwrites the layer and always produces grayscale output regardless of original image colors
pi_items.source: Official GIMP documentation states: 'Grayscale Output: This noise is always gray, even if you applied it to a very colorful image—the filter completely overwrites any existing background in the layer'

pi_items.metadata_value: Solid Noise filter supports X size and Y size parameters
pi_items.verified_value: X size and Y size parameters exist and control noise shape dimensions with range 0.1 to 16.0
pi_items.source: Official GIMP 2.10 documentation at https://docs.gimp.org/2.10/en/gimp-filter-noise-solid.html confirms X and Y size parameters with range 0.1 to 16.0

pi_items.verified_value: Environment configured for GIMP 2.10 (setup scripts reference .config/GIMP/2.10 directory structure)
pi_items.source: setup_gimp.sh creates directory structure: mkdir -p "$home_dir/.config/GIMP/2.10" and install_gimp.sh installs 'gimp' package from apt repositories

privileged_info_summary: The solid_noise task generates a 512x512 white canvas and instructs users to apply GIMP's Solid Noise render filter (Filters → Render → Clouds → Solid Noise in GIMP 2.10). The Solid Noise filter generates procedural grayscale noise textures and supports parameters: X size/Y size (range 0.1-16.0), Detail (range 0-15), plus Tileable and Turbulent checkboxes. The task data is entirely synthetic, generated on-the-fly during task execution. The setup script contains inaccurate parameter descriptions: claiming Turbulence controls smoothness with range 1-7 (when it's a checkbox), and Detail range as 1-15 (when the actual range is 0-15).
